action.content: Write a 30-line poem with short sentences without any comma. Each line should contain exactly one sentence. Make sure that you put the right punctuation at the end of each line. Your entire response should contain the poem only.
action.label: context

action.content:
The sun rises high above the hill.
Birds sing their morning songs so clear.
The wind blows gently through the trees.
Clouds drift slowly across the blue sky.
A small dog runs across the grass.
A cat sits quietly on a wall.
Children play in the warm afternoon light.
Shadows stretch long on the sandy beach.
The ocean waves crash against the shore.
Seagulls cry out from the rocky cliffs.
Fish swim deep beneath the water surface.
A boat floats peacefully on the waves.
The air smells fresh and full of life.
Butterflies dance among the colorful flowers.
A bee hums near the blooming bush.
Rain begins to fall in soft drops.
The ground becomes wet and cool to touch.
Thunder rumbles low in the distant sky.
Lightning flashes bright across the horizon.
The storm passes with a sudden end.
The sun returns to warm the earth again.
Leaves glisten with fresh morning dew.
The world feels new and full of hope.
Time moves forward without any pause.
We watch the day begin and end.
Nature repeats its ancient, gentle song.
Life continues in every small way.
The earth spins on its quiet axis.
Tomorrow brings another chance to see.
The cycle of life goes on forever.
action.label: response
